Summary: Uninstalling a MATLAB App containing folders will leave empty folders behind.
Description: Uninstalling an app that contains extra folders for MATLAB code or data will remove all files, but will leave the folders intact. The remaining folders are harmless and can be removed manually.
Workaround: There is no workaround.

Summary: Error during code generation when File packaging format set to Compact and Shared code placement set to Auto
Description: In some models using MATLAB structures to store parameters or bus signals without bus objects, you may encounter an error during code generation similar to the following: Error: Error occurred when generating shared type: Type, 'struct_EF6jG8VvmjOPAZSjmOw0PE', is specified to export to a header file, but compact file format has been requested. Either select a different file packaging format or do not select the 'Export' option for the data type. This happens under the following conditions: On the Code Generation > Code Placement  pane, the File packaging format  option is set to Compact  On the Code Generation > Interface  pane, the Shared code placement  option is set to Auto  The model is a standalone model (not part of a model reference hierarchy)
Workaround: Do one of the following:In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Code Generation > Interface pane, set the Shared code placement option to Shared location.On the Code Generation > Code Placement pane, set the File packaging format option to Modular.If you can identify the bus without a bus object that is generating the structure type, create a bus object for that signal.If you can identify the MATLAB structure parameter that is generating the structure type, replace the parameter with a Simulink data object.??

Summary: Error: Unexpected exception SysErr (SysErr: No such file or directory [noent]) when downloading results from Polyspace Metrics
Description: When downloading results through the Polyspace Metrics Web interface, you may see the following error: Error: Unexpected exception SysErr (SysErr: No such file or directory [noent])  This error occurs on a client machine. During the download, Polyspace tries to create temporary files in the results repository folder. The error is produced because the results repository folder does not exist on a client machine.
Workaround: On a client machine where Polyspace is installed in the default folder c:\Program Files\Polyspace\PolyspaceForCandCPP_R2013a, do the following:From a DOS command window, run c:\Program Files\Polyspace\PolyspaceForCandCPP_R2013a\polyspace\bin\polyspaced.exe -check-only From your Polyspace Metrics Web page, download the results again.

Summary: The order in which certain cDAQ module types are added to a Session may result in an error
Description: The order in which cDAQ devices are added to a session may result in an error under these conditions: One of the devices does not support on-demand operations and the other does. The device that supports on-demand operations is added to a Session before   you add the device that does not support on-demand operations.  If you add a device that does not support on-demand operations to the session first, you will not see an error: >> s = daq.createSession('ni');  >> s.addAnalogInputChannel('cDAQ1Mod2','ai0','Voltage');  Warning: A channel that does not support on-demand operations was added to the session. On-demand operations using inputSingleScan and outputSingleScan will be disabled. Only clocked operations using startForeground and startBackground can be done. Warning: The Rate property was increased to 1651.6129 due to changes in the session configuration.  >> s.addAnalogInputChannel('cDAQ1Mod1 ','ai0','Voltage'); If you add a device that supports on-demand operations first, you get this error: >> s = daq.createSession('ni'); >> s.addAnalogInputChannel('cDAQ1Mod1 ','ai0','Voltage'); >> s.addAnalogInputChannel('cDAQ1Mod2 ','ai0','Voltage');  NI Error -200077: Requested value is not a supported value for this property. The property value may be invalid because it conflicts with another property.Property: DAQmx_SampTimingType Requested Value: DAQmx_Val_OnDemand You Can Select: DAQmx_Val_SampClkDevice: cDAQ1Task Name: _unnamedTask<39>Status Code: -200077??
Workaround: This issue is resolved in R2013b. If you are using previous releases,  add devices to Session in this order:First add devices that do not support on-demand operations and can only perform clocked operations.Then add devices that do provide support for on-demand operations.If you continue to see the error and need technical assistance, contact MathWorks technical support: https://www.mathworks.com/support/service_requests/contact_support.do

Summary: Plot block and Simulation Viewer show incorrect results.
Description: When plotting simulation results in the simulation viewer or in a plot block of a model diagram, the displayed results for a species will be incorrect if the following conditions are met: The species is in concentration (as controlled by its InitialAmountUnits  property or by the DefaultSpeciesDimension  property of the Simulation Settings), and The species is not selected in the Data Logging section of the Simulation Settings.  Under these conditions, the plot displays the amount of the species instead of the concentration. However, all simulation results that are logged are correctly reported both in all plots and on the results page.
Workaround: When you plot a species in the Simulation Viewer or in a plot block, ensure that you also select that species in the Data Logging section of the Simulation Settings.

Summary: Passing vector-valued physical signals with more than 10 elements through Simulink-PS Converter block may result in signal order being intermixed
Description: This bug appears when a vector-valued signal that has more than 10 scalar elements is being passed through a Simulink-PS Converter block. This bug does not appear when vector-valued physical signals are passed between Simscape blocks or passed through a PS-Simulink Converter block. The bug manifests in rearrangement of the individual elements of the vector signal.
Workaround: To work around this issue, split signals into vectors of less than 10 elements and pass them through separate Simulink-PS Converter blocks.

Summary: Optimtool sometimes errors when importing fminunc or lsqlin options
Description: If you try to import a set of optimization options for fminunc  or lsqlin  into the Optimization Tool, you can receive an error. This error occurs when either: The options were created using optimoptions  and the Algorithm  option was not set by user . The options were exported from the Optimization Tool in R2013a and the Algorithm  option was not altered in Optimization Tool.
Workaround: Update the options before importing. If myoptions is the name of your options, then execute at the MATLAB command line:myoptions.Algorithm = myoptions.Algorithm;This marks the Algorithm option as being set by user. You can now import the options into the Optimization Tool.?

Summary: Error from Simulink Project when committing a changeset with double quotes in the comment
Description: On Windows, when using Command-Line SVN Integration in Simulink Project, if you try to check in a changeset with double quotes in the comment, the commit fails and you see an error like the following. Could not commit files:svn commit -m "some comment "somthing in double quotes""" [path]Could not be executed with Error Message:svn: E155007: [some text] is not a working copy
Workaround: To avoid this problem, replace " with \" in the comment.

Summary: Code inspection fails for signal data objects with imported storage classes
Description: If both of the following are true, a model compatible with Simulink Code Inspector can fail code inspection: The model contains a signal data object with an imported storage class. Imported storage classes include ImportedExtern  and ImportedExternPointer  or custom storage classes with Data scope  set to Imported.  In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Optimization  pane, neither Remove root level I/O zero initialization  or Remove internal data zero initialization  are selected.   ?
Workaround: Consider one of the following:If the signal with the imported storage connects to a root level Inport or Outport block, in the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Optimization pane, select Remove root level I/O zero initialization.If the signal with the imported storage class connects to a non-root level block, in the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Optimization pane, select Remove internal data zero initialization.?

Description: Under rare circumstances, MATLAB might crash when generating code for a Stateflow chart containing Atomic Subcharts. The generated stack trace contains the error message: CGIR assertion 'rtwInterface->GetNumDWorkVars() >= numDWorks' failed in '.\cdr\cdr_rtw_interface.cpp:1169'

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Reusable subsystem with multiple outputs driving merge block might cause wrong answers
Description: Reusable subsystem with multiple outputs driving merge block might cause wrong answers in a model where in the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Optimization > Signals and Parameters  pane, the Pass reusable subsystem outputs passed as  is set to Individual arguments . Subsystem reuse might occur for the following two subsystems: The first reusable subsystem has multiple outputs driving a Merge block The second reusable subsystem does not have multiple outputs driving a Merge block  For the two reusable subsystems, a function might be generated with one output argument and might be reused for a subsystem that expects multiple output arguments. This can result in wrong answers. ?
Workaround: In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Optimization > Signals and Parameters pane, set the Pass reusable subsystem outputs passed as  to  Structure reference.

Summary: Model Reference RTW Target and top-level build may unnecessarily rebuild when Simulink data object naming rules are used
Description: When a value other than "None" is selected for "Signal naming" or "Parameter naming" on Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Symbols > Simulink data object naming rules, the model reference RTW target or top-level build may rebuild, even when there are no changes to the model or its dependencies.

Summary: There is a typo error in the Task 1 (Set Up XMakefile Configuration) of  'Code Generation and Verification' demo help of vxworks.
Description: The 'Code Generation and Verification' demo of vxworks fails when you follow the steps mentioned in the demo help under the path: Simulink Coder->Simulink Coder Examples ->VxWorks 6.x Real-Time Operating System. This failure is because of missing double quotes in the Post-build command of xmakefilesetup.
Workaround: In the Task 1, step2 (Set Up XMakefile Configuration) of 'Code Generation and Verification' demo of  vxworks, the arguments mentioned for Post-build tool is as follows. rtpSp \"host:[|||MW_XMK_GENERATED_TARGET_REF[E]|||]\">"[|||MW_XMK_OUTPUT_PATH_REF[E]|||]vxscript.txt"Replace the above command with the following.rtpSp \""host:[|||MW_XMK_GENERATED_TARGET_REF[E]|||]\"">"[|||MW_XMK_OUTPUT_PATH_REF[E]|||]vxscript.txt"

Summary: MATLAB process crashes on Mac OS X and Linux when calling MATLAB Engine API engGetVariable to get a MATLAB object.
Description: The MATLAB Engine API function, engGetVariable , does not support MATLAB object data types. On Mac OS X and Linux platforms, MATLAB crashes when you call engGetVariable  for a MATLAB object. The following example creates an MException  object, ex . The MATLAB process crashes when you call engGetVariable  on ex . #include <engine.h>#include <stdio.h>int main() {    Engine *eng = NULL;    mxArray *foo;    int result;    eng = engOpen(NULL);    if(eng==NULL) {        fprintf(stderr, &quot;Can't start MATLAB engine\n&quot;);        return(1);    }    result = engEvalString(eng, "ex=MException('a:b','c');");     printf("result of engEvalString: %d\n", result);     foo = engGetVariable(eng, "ex"); // this crashes the MATLAB process    engClose(eng);    return 0;} This crash does not happen on Windows platforms ? ?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect data type conversion (cast) operator replacement with AUTOSAR 4.0 code replacement library
Description: During code generation for a model for which the AUTOSAR 4.0 code replacement library (CRL) is selected, an incorrect operator replacement might occur if both of the following conditions exist: The model contains a data type conversion (cast) operation, due to the presence of a Data Type Conversion block or another block, such as an addition block, that performs a data type conversion. The data type of the data type conversion input or output is fixed-point and has a non-unary slope adjustment factor.  Under these conditions, the data type conversion operator might be replaced with an incorrect AUTOSAR data type conversion function and the slope adjustment factor might be omitted from the code. Additionally, a correct operator replacement might occur, but with an incorrect exponent input value, if all of the following conditions exist: The model contains a data type conversion (cast) operation (as above). The data type of the data type conversion input is floating-point (f32 ). The data type of the data type conversion output is fixed-point and has a unary slope adjustment factor.  Under these conditions, the data conversion operator is correctly replaced with an AUTOSAR data type conversion function (Mfl_Cvrt_f32_outtype  ), but the generated input value is the fixed exponent of the output data type rather than the expected value -1*FixedExponent  .

Summary: When you check the "Minimize algebraic loop occurrences" option for an atomic subsystem, the sorted order of the blocks inside this subsystem may be incorrect. This can lead to incorrect simulation results.
Description: In the attached model, a root inport block connects to the sum block(Add1). The sum block is also in a loop completed by 1/z and product block. In such a construction, the minimizing algebraic loop algorithms will cause a problem because the subsystem is marked as non-direct feed-through but the 1/z block has not been moved into a synthesized atomic subsystem.
Workaround: Option 1: To work around this problem, unselect the "Minimize algebraic loop occurrences" option for the subsystem. This may cause Simulink to report an algebraic loop. Remove this loop by adding an unit delay or memory block in the loop.

Summary: dsolve behaves unexpectedly when called for differential equations in terms of the dependent ODE variable D(t).
Description: These commands in MATLAB return no solution: syms D(t)dsolve(diff(D) == 1)  Instead, they issue an unexpected warning message: Warning: The result cannot be displayed due a previously interrupted computation or out of memory. Run 'reset(symengine)' and rerun the commands to regenerate the result. > In sym.disp at 29 In sym.display at 28
Workaround: Avoid using dependent ODE variables D(t) or DD(t). D is a reserved name in the MuPAD symbolic engine. The best approach is to avoid using reserved keywords as variable or function names. For example, the commandssyms Y(t)dsolve(diff(Y) == 1)work as expected.

Summary: Static Code Metrics Report might provide inconsistent function metrics in Table view
Description: On the Code Generation Report, on the Static Code Metrics Report  pane, the function metrics in the Function Information  section might differ between the Call Tree and Table views. The Call Tree view provides the correct metrics.

Summary: MPS .NET client does not allow simultaneous execution of MATLAB functions
Description: Users of the MATLAB Production Server (MPS) .NET client can create multiple threads in their .NET client application and simultaneously invoke MATLAB functions from these threads. The expectation is that these MATLAB functions will get executed in parallel, reducing the execution time. However, due to a bug in the MPS .NET client code, these MATLAB function calls from multiple threads are serialized and not executed simultaneously.
Workaround: This issue has been fixed in the attached version of MPS .NET client assembly. Please unzip the attached mps_patch.zip to get the updated version of MPS .NET client assembly (MathWorks.MATLAB.ProductionServer.Client.dll). This assembly should work with both R2012b and R2013a versions of MATLAB Production Server.

Description: sldvmergeharness command incorrectly handles configured parameters specified by parameter configuration feature of Simulink Design Verifier. For the following use of sldvmergeharness command: sldvmergeharness('merged_model',{'input_model_a','input_model_b'})  the incorrect handling of the specified parameters is caused with the following conditions: No parameter configuration is applied to input_model_a  (the 1st model of the 2nd argument). Summary: Test generation stops, reports incompatibility error for model using configuration reference when set to ignore objectives satisfied in existing coverage data
Description: Test generation stops during compilation and reports an incompatibility error for a model that has an active configuration reference and that contains one or more Model blocks. This occurs when: The referenced configuration set does not contain Simulink Design Verifier configuration parameters. In the model, you enable Design Verifier  > Options  > Ignore objectives satisfied in existing coverage data .
Workaround: When analyzing your model with Simulink Design Verifier, if your model contains one or more Model blocks, do not use configuration set references.

Summary: Error message internal error: (at end of source) assertion failed: unreachable code during compilation stage
Description: You might see this error if your code has a static  const  variable that is defined with a ternary operator in the main()  procedure.  As this kind of error is code-specific, it is not possible to give a code example that reproduces the problem.
Workaround: Use one of the following workarounds:Remove the static const variable if it is not necessary.Reposition the static const variable so that it has file scope.

Summary: If a block uses a semicolon ';' to specify a parameter, code inspection may exit with a segmentation violation

Summary: High memory usage with models using constant parameters generated to shared location
Description: Models and model reference hierarchies that use many constant parameters and/or large constant parameters might generate a large const_params.c  file. This can cause high memory usage, which might result in MATLAB running out of memory. ?
Workaround: For a model without any referenced models, on the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Code Generation > Interface pane, set the Shared code placement option to Auto. This prevents constants from being generated to the shared location.You can also use the attached MATLAB script to prevent generation of constants to the shared location. After following the installation instructions, a script named disable_shared_constants is installed to the <MATLAB>/bugreports/disable_shared_constants folder. Add this folder to the MATLAB path and run disable_shared_constants. This stops shared constants from being generated for the remainder of that MATLAB session. The disable_shared_constants folder may also be copied or moved to a different location.Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting Run as administrator.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: MATLAB Engine application crashes on Mac OSX and Linux when passing NULL to Engine APIs as the pointer of Engine
Description: MATLAB Engine APIs engEvalString, engGetVariable, engPutVariable, and engClose expect a pointer of MATLAB Engine as their first parameter. If a NULL pointer is passed to those functions as the first parameter, the MATLAB Engine application will crash on Mac OSX and Linux. Following example Engine appliaction will crash due to the statement engEvalString(NULL, "version"). #include "engine.h"#include <stdio.h>int main(){     Engine *eng=NULL;     eng=engOpen("");     if(eng==NULL) {             fprintf(stderr, "Can't start MATLAB engine\n");             return(1);     }     engEvalString(NULL,"version");     engClose(eng);     return 0;} This MATLAB Engine application does not crash on Windows platforms. ? ? ? ?

Summary: Outer join on dataset arrays returns merged key variables in wrong order
Description: When you perform an outer join between two dataset  arrays with the 'MergeKeys',true  option, the output array might have some of the values for the merged key variables in the wrong order. If there are key values in the "right" input that are not matched by key values in the "left" input, then the key values in the output array for those observations come from the "right" input. If the key variables are specified in an order different from how they appear in the "left" input, those values are in the wrong variables in the output array. For example, the values of V1  and V2  in the last observation in the dataset array joined  below should be swapped: >> left = dataset([1;2;3],[11;12;13],randn(3,1),'VarNames',{'V1' 'V2' 'V3'})left =     V1   V2   V3     1    11   1.409    2    12   1.4172    3    13   0.6715 >> right = dataset([2;3;4],[12;13;14],randn(3,1),'VarNames',{'V1' 'V2' 'V4'})right =     V1   V2   V4     2    12   -1.2075    3    13   0.71724    4    14   1.6302 >> joined = join(left,right,'Type','outer','Keys',{'V2' 'V1'},'MergeKeys',true)joined =     V1   V2   V3      V4     1    11   1.409   NaN    2    12   1.4172  -1.2075    3    13   0.6715  0.71724    14   4    NaN     1.6302
Workaround: Specify the key variables in their original order, for example:>> joined = join(left,right,'Type','outer','Keys',{'V1' 'V2'},'MergeKeys',true)joined =     V1   V2   V3      V4     1    11   1.409   NaN    2    12   1.4172  -1.2075    3    13   0.6715  0.71724    4    14   NaN     1.6302

Summary: Code generation fails with the message "assertion failed" and a stack trace mentioning "ReusePolicyUB::getPrecisionLoss".
Description: Code generation fails with the message "assertion failed" and a stack trace mentioning "ReusePolicyUB::getPrecisionLoss". This failure is due to a bug in the computation of upper bounds for variable-sized matrices in the MATLAB?  code.
Workaround: Use dynamic memory allocation for all variable-sized arrays:At the command line, create a code generation configuration object and set the DynamicMemoryAllocation parameter to 'AllVariableSizeArrays'.       x = coder.config('lib');        x.DynamicMemoryAllocation='AllVariableSizeArrays'       codegen -config x ...In a project, on the Project Settings dialog box Memory tab, set Dynamic memory allocation to For all variable-sized arrays.There is no workaround for this problem when using the MATLAB Function Block in Simulink?.

Summary: Incorrect initialization code for complex matrix parameter containing nonfinite values
Description: The code generator might generate incorrect initialization code when all of the following conditions for a model are met: A signal or parameter is a matrix or vector The element type of the matrix or vector is complex The initial value contains non-finite values  When this occurs, you see different results from running a simulation on the model than from running the generated code.

Summary: Always create a code generation report option might cause code generation error
Description: On the Project Settings > Debugging  pane, if you select the Always create a code generation report  check box, an error might occur during code generation: Compilation failed.Assertion failed.Use help codegen for more information on using this command.
Workaround: Do one of the following:Locate files named tmp.m in your MATLAB search path by running which tmp.m at the command line. Rename any files found.On the Project Settings > Debugging pane, clear the Always create a code generation report check box.

Summary: With serial architectures, incorrect code is generated for SOS filters when 'Coefficient source' is selected as 'Processor interface'
Description: When you generate HDL code for SOS filters with serial architectures and you select 'CoefficientSource' as the processor interface, the generated code has the port interface for the processor but the RTL logic in the module does not use the coefficients from the port. The logic uses internal fixed coefficients instead.
Workaround: There is no workaround. Serial architectures currently do not support processor interface for IIR SOS filters.

Summary: Signal object with initial value might overwrite the zero value initial condition of the connecting Unit Delay block
Description: The code generator might generate incorrect code when all of the following conditions are met: A signal object with non-zero initial value is associated with the input signal of a Unit Delay block. The Unit Delay block has a zero value initial condition. In the generated code, the variable for the input signal can also be used as the state of the unit delay if the two have the same initial value.  For an example, see the attached model mSignalObjectBug.slx . In the generated code, the variable for the input signal is incorrectly reused as the unit delay state.
Workaround: Download the attached MATLAB script WorkaroundForBugReport916452.p.To enable the workaround, execute the script WorkaroundForBugReport916452(true).To disable the workaround, execute the script WorkaroundForBugReport916452(false).

Summary: Incorrect data type conversion error or warning during simulation
Description: When you convert fixed-point data types such as sfix8_En2  to data types with larger ranges, such as double , Simulink incorrectly reports that a downcast has occurred and triggers an error or warning.
Workaround: You can avoid seeing the incorrect diagnostic warning or error by performing the following actions.In the Configuration Parameters dialog, select Diagnostics > Data Validity.In the Parameters section, set Detect downcast to none.

Summary: Model and referenced model using global Goto/From blocks incorrectly reported incompatible with Simulink Design Verifier
Description: If your model uses global Goto and From blocks and references another model that uses global Goto and From blocks, Simulink Design Verifier incorrectly reports that your model is incompatible. This situation only occurs when there are no conflicting Goto/From labels between the model and any of its referenced models.

Summary: Signal label propagation for buses that feed Goto blocks increases model update time
Description: Enabling signal label propagation for bus signals that feed a large number of Goto blocks increases model update time significantly.
Workaround: The workaround for Bug Report 827771 also works for this bug.

Summary: Packet Output and Stream Output blocks do not work correctly with enabled subsystems
Description: When a Packet Output or Stream Output block is placed inside an enabled subsystem, it is not disabled when the subsystem is disabled, but continues to send messages.
Workaround: As a workaround:Log into your computer using an account with Administrator privileges.Look at the list of available workaround files, named according to the release they support.Select and download the workaround file for your release.Locate the directory of the downloaded file. This directory is referred to as <DOWNLOAD> in subsequent steps.Type matlabroot to locate your MATLAB installation. This directory is referred to as <MATLABROOT> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Navigate to <DOWNLOAD> and open the downloaded file.Extract the contents of the file to <MATLABROOT>.Restart MATLAB.At the MATLAB command prompt, type rtwintgt -setup to reinstall the Real-Time Windows Target kernel.January 31, 2013: attachment_915390_12b_win32_2013-01-31.zip: works on R2012b Win32 onlyJanuary 31, 2013: attachment_915390_12b_win64_2013-01-31.zip: works on R2012b Win64 only

Summary: Incorrect last-modified time and date for copied files in the Current Folder browser
Description: When you copy a file from outside the Current Folder browser, the current time and date are incorrectly displayed as the file's last-modified time and date. For example, the Date Modified field is displayed incorrectly in the Current Folder browser when you drag and drop a file from Windows Explorer.

Summary: The Box-Cox transformation filters out too many observation points
Description: The Box-Cox transformation, in some cases, overcautiously filters out observations to ensure that the transformation does not introduce rounding errors. This can happen when Lambda is very small (e.g., -2.5) and the magnitude of the data points is quite large (e.g. 300).

Summary: Simulink.Bus.getLeafBusElements modifies bus objects and might return incorrect values
Description: Using Simulink.Bus.getLeafBusElements  on a bus object can modify the object in the workspace in the following ways: Remove the 'Bus: ' string from the DataType  field for all elements of bus type Change the name of the bus object listed within the DataType  field  When the name of the bus object is changed, Simulink.Bus.getLeafBusElements  mistakes the bus object for an element and stops traversing through the bus hierarchy. This will return incorrect results.
Workaround: Use the attached function findLeafElements instead of Simulink.Bus.getLeafBusElements. This function returns an array of bus elements.

Summary: The latex function returns unexpected results for the arguments sym('PI'), sym('E'), and sym('I').
Description: latex(sym('PI'))latex(sym('I'))latex(sym('E')) return ans =\mathrm{PI}_{V}\mathrm{ar} ans =I_{V}\mathrm{ar} ans =E_{V}\mathrm{ar} The artifacts _{V}\mathrm{ar}  are unexpected.
Workaround: Remove the artifacts _{V}\mathrm{ar} using the core MATLAB function strrep. For example,str = latex(sym('PI') + sym('I') + sym('E'))returnsstr =E_{V}\mathrm{ar} + I_{V}\mathrm{ar} + \mathrm{PI}_{V}\mathrm{ar}Use strrep to remove _{V}\mathrm{ar}:strrep(str,'_{V}\mathrm{ar}','')ans =E + I + \mathrm{PI}

Summary: C_CodeMetrics related depfun error when compiling code with mcc or deploytool
Description: When trying to compile MATLAB code, the following error is encountered: Depfun error: 'The specified superclass 'rtw.codemetrics.C_CodeMetrics' contains a parse error or cannot be found on MATLAB's search path, possibly shadowed by another file with the same name.' Error using mcc Error executing mcc, return status = 1 (0x1).  The error message points to C_CodeMetrics  functionality which is a part of the Simulink Coder toolbox.  However, this error is encountered despite having no code related to Simulink Coder.
Workaround: Remove all toolboxes from the depfun search path, and then re-add the specific toolboxes that your MATLAB code utilizes.To do this with mcc, use the -N option to clear all toolboxes, and then the -p option to add each specific toolbox.  For example, to clear all toolboxes and then add the Image Processing Toolbox and the Image Acquisition Toolbox, do:mcc -N -p images -p imaq -m foo.mThe names such as "images" and "imaq" that refer to the toolboxes follow the folder naming convention found in the matlabroot/toolbox directory, where matlabroot is the path to your MATLAB installation root.To do this with deploytool, open settings in the deploytool window (gear button in the top right corner), go to the "Toolboxes On Path" tab, clear all toolboxes, and then add the toolboxes you are using.

Summary: Matrix expressions as model arguments produce incorrect results in normal simulation mode
Description: Simulink produces incorrect results when you use a matrix expression such as [a, b; c, d]  to specify a model argument to a submodel. The incorrect results occur when all the following conditions are met. The expression represents a two-dimensional matrix of double-precision real numbers. The expression contains at least one variable. In the top model, the Inline Parameter  property is set to On . Simulation mode is set to Normal Mode .  Under these conditions, the submodel receives a transposed matrix from its model argument. Therefore, in the submodel, [a, b; c, d]  becomes [a, c; b, d] .
Workaround: Instead of directly using an expresssion as a model argument, assign the expression to a variable in your workspace. Then, assign that variable as the model argument.

Summary: textscan throws "std::exception" message when string or file starts with newline characters
Description: The textscan  function throws a "std::exception" message when the string or file starts with multiple newline characters. For example, this code:textscan(sprintf('\n\n'), '%s') Returns the following message:Caught "std::exception" Exception message is: invalid string position

Summary: Unexpected reshaping occurs when indexing symbolic vectors into multidimensional arrays
Description: When executing the commands syms x y; a = [x, y]; b(:,:,1) = a; size(b) the size of b  is [2,1]  instead of the expected [1,2] . This behavior breaks consistency with indexing in core MATLAB.

Summary: cfirpm errors out with:  Undefined function or variable "HH_n"
Description: Using cfirpm  to design an arbitrary-response FIR equiripple filter errors when the initial minimax approximation error is small. An initial approximation error that is too small causes the iterative algorithm to not execute and results in the error: Undefined function or variable "HH_n".

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: When targeting TI C2834x processors, the default memory mapping for the IQMath code section incorrectly points to IQFPUTABLES leading to a linking error of the generated code when IQMath functions are used.
Description: When using IQMath functions in your model configured for TI C2834x, either by placing IQMath blocks in your model or by using the c28x Code Replacement Library (CRL), the default mapping of the IQMath code section generates a linking error, as it incorrectly points to IQFPUTABLES.The IQMath code section needs to point to a RAM memory bank available on the processor.
Workaround: Workaround for this issue is to change the default mapping for the IQMath code section in the Target Preferences.Target Preferences are accessible by double clicking on the specific block available in releases prior to R2013a. In R2013a, Target Hardware Resources are accessible from the Configuration Parameters of the model under Code Generation > Coder Target. On the "Section" tab of the Target Preferences or Target Hardware Resources, change the assignment of the IQMath section to one of the RAM memory bank (RAMM0, RAML0L1, RAMH0H1).

Summary: Square Root block with Fixed-Point Data Types Might Introduce Unexpected Overflow
Description: The results of the Square Root block might overflow incorrectly for the following conditions:1. The input and output data types are fixed-point data types, AND2. The intermediate data type is not set to be 'Inherit via internal rule'
Workaround: Set the intermediate data type to be 'Inherit via internal rule'.

Summary: The PWM Generator (2-Level) block of Control and Measurements library of powerlib does not work as expected
Description: There is an error in the way the block is implemented when the Generator type  parameter is set to Single-phase full-bridge (4 pulses) . The block provides incorrect simulation results.
Workaround: To work around the issue, copy and paste this code in the MATLAB Command window: open_system('powerlib_meascontrol'); set_param('powerlib_meascontrol','lock','off'); blk = 'powerlib_meascontrol/Pulse & Signal Generators/PWM Generator (2-Level)/Full bridge (1-ph)/Relational Operator3';set_param(blk','operator','>='); save_system('powerlib_meascontrol'); close_system('powerlib_meascontrol');

Summary: Simulink can report Data Store Read-Write timing diagnostics incorrectly for multi-rate Data Store Memory
Description: If a Data Store Memory has readers and writers in different tasks (i.e. with different sample times in a model with a Multi-Tasking solver mode), Simulink can incorrectly report the three Data Store Memory Block diagnostics: Detect read before write Detect write after read Detect write after write   as follows: Detect read before write: If the Data Store Write has a faster sample time than the Data Store Read, Simulink can incorrectly report that a read operation is happening before a write operation. Detect write after read: If the Data Store Read has a faster sample time than the corresponding Data Store Write, Simulink cannot detect a write after read that it otherwise should. Detect write after write: If two Data Store Writes have different sample times and both write at the same simulation time, Simulink cannot detect a write after write when it should. ?

Summary: On Linux? platforms, there are formatting issues when viewing Microsoft? Word .docx qualification artifacts with OpenOffice.
Description: On Linux platforms, the Certification Artifacts Explorer uses OpenOffice to view Microsoft? Word .docx  qualification artifacts. When viewing the artifacts, you might encounter formatting issues with page breaks, link breaks, or tables.
Workaround: 1. Save the .docx file as an .odt file, using Microsoft? Word on a Windows? platform.2. Open the .odt version of the artifact, using OpenOffice on a Linux? platform.

Summary: Simulation And Code Generation Mismatch: Simulation and code generation initial condition results do not match when a Model block drives a conditionally executed subsystem Outport block under certain conditions.
Description: The simulation and code generation results do not match for a model when all the following conditions hold true: The Initial value  of the conditionally executed subsystem Outport block is 0 . This Outport block is driven by an output port of a Model block. In the model referenced by the Model block, the root-level Outport block corresponding to the above output port of the Model block is driven by an IC block whose Initial value differs from 0.  In the generated code, the conditionally executed subsystem output is incorrectly initialized with the initial value of the IC block in the referenced model instead of the initial value of 0, which is specified in the subsystem Outport block dialog. The attached .zip file contains a model which exhibits this behavior.
Workaround: To work around this issue, place a Signal Conversion block between the output port of the Model block and the Outport block of the conditionally executed subsystem.

Summary: Using copyContentsToSubSystem to copy self-modifying subsystems can crash MATLAB
Description: Calling Simulink.BlockDiagram.copyContentsToSubSystem  on a subsystem that modifies itself or contains a subsystem that modifies itself can cause MATLAB to crash. Examples of self-modifying subsystems are subsystems that add or delete blocks in a callback or mask initialization.
Workaround: In the Simulink Editor, select the subsystem contents that you wish to copy. Use copy and paste to copy the contents to the destination block diagram.

Summary: Referenced model containing subsystem with Reusable function packaging can result in uncompilable code
Description: If you have a referenced model containing an inner subsystem with Function packaging  set to Reusable function , it is possible for code generation to fail to define a run-time model variable. As a result:- When attempting to compile the generated code, the compiler can report errors. - Without this variable, it is possible that the subsystem cannot use features such as logging which access model-specific information.
Workaround: For each inner subsystem in your referenced model, do the following:- In the Subsystem Parameters dialog box, on the Code Generation tab, select Function packaging as Inline or Nonreusable

Summary: Models that use I/O interrupts can crash a target computer that uses a i5/i7 CPU.
Description: Models that use I/O interrupts to receive/send data can crash a target computer that uses an i5/i7 CPU. The crash occurs because the floating-point structure related instruction xrstor generates a general protection exception.

Summary: Possible memory corruption when MATLAB? code calls a MATLAB? Coder? generated MEX function that has read-only inputs?
Description: MATLAB can crash when MATLAB code calls a MATLAB Coder generated MEX function that has read-only inputs. Optimizations during processing of the read-only inputs can corrupt memory.
Workaround: Suppose your MATLAB code calls a MATLAB Coder generated MEX function:y = my_function_mex(a, b, c);Assign  temporary variables to the read-only input values and call the MEX function using the temporary variables:t1 = a;t2 = b;t3 = c;y = my_function_mex(t1, t2, t3);?

Summary: Test and Measurement Tool creates spurious TCPIP/UDP connections
Description: If you create a UDP or TCP/IP connection in Test and Measurement Tool (TMTOOL) and if you close TMTOOL, every time you open and close TMTOOL the number of connections doubles. You can also not remove the connections correctly and you may receive a JAVA exception in the command-line window (in MATLAB). Also the UDP/TCPIP connection information is saved in tmtool.cfg, which is located in the preference directory. As these spurious connections increase, the size of the tmtool.cfg also increases, thus causing significant delays in starting TMTOOL. ?
Workaround: When you observe spurious UDP/TCPIP connections in TMTOOL do the following:1. Go to the MATLAB preference directory. From the MATLAB command prompt, execute the following command:cd(prefdir)2. Manually delete the tmtool.cfg in the preference directory. Please note that deleting the tmtool.cfg file will erase the history of devices/instruments that you have connected to using TMTOOL. If you need that information, copy it to another file before deleting the config file.

Summary: Simulating a model with Model blocks can give error indicating that a block "does not have port handles"
Description: This unexpected error message can appear when top-level models are configured to log signals that are no longer present in referenced models. For example, suppose you have a model, topMdl, which references a model, refMdl. Suppose further that refMdl originally had a sybsystem, oldSubsystem, with signals that were configured to log in topMdl, and oldSubsystem is deleted. If the DataLoggingOverride  model parameter for topModel contains signals that are no longer present in the referenced model because they were deleted when oldSubsystem was deleted, you may see the following error upon simulation: Unable to get the signal name for a Simulink.SimulationData.SignalLoggingInfo object because'refMdl/oldSubsystem' does not have port handles.  Caused by:  Invalid Simulink object name: refMdl/oldSubsystem
Workaround: To work around this issue, refresh any stale DataLoggingOverride parameters. For the example, in the Bug Description section above:>> dlo = get_param('topMdl', 'DataLoggingOverride');>> dlo = dlo.validate(model, true, true, false, 'warnAndRemove'); >> set_param('topMdl', 'DataLoggingOverride', dlo);

Summary: The RS Encoder and Decoder blocks do not allow N-K to be odd, where N represents the codeword length and K represents the message length.
Description: The following blocks do not allow the specification of a code in which N-K is odd, where N represents the codeword length and K represents the message length: Integer-Input RS Encoder Integer-Output RS Decoder Binary-Input RS Encoder Binary-Output RS Decoder  In addition, the following System objects do not allow that same code specification: comm.RSEncoder comm.RSDecoder
Workaround: Prior to R2011a, edit the file <matlabroot>/toolbox/commblks/commmasks/commblkbchrs.m. In R2011a and after, edit the file <matlabroot>/toolbox/comm/commutilities/comminit/commblkbchrs.m.In commblkbchrs.m, navigate to the checkNKCombo subfunction.Find the following line of code in that subfunction: if floor(t) ~= t || t<1Change that line to: if t<1Find the line of code in that subfunction that contains "N and K must differ by a positive even integer"Change that string to "The difference of N and K must be at least 2"

Summary: Clearing a MEX function generated from code containing a parfor-loop can cause MATLAB? to stop
Description: Clearing a MEX function generated from code containing a parfor -loop can cause MATLAB? to stop under these circumstances: You generate the MEX function using MATLAB Coder? with the Linux? GCC compiler.  After you run and clear the MEX function, you use a command, such as ls , which uses the Linux fork function.   A MEX function generated from code containing a parfor- loop uses the OpenMP library. Running the MEX function loads the OpenMP library. Clearing the MEX function unloads the OpenMP library. On Linux systems, loading and unloading the OpenMP library makes the use of the fork function by subsequent commands unreliable.
Workaround: Download and run the attached MEX function, OpenMPFix, before running a MEX function generated by MATLAB Coder? from code containing a parfor-loop.

Summary: The electrical torque Te signal of Synchronous Machine SI Fundamental block with mechanical rotational port is incorrectly measured in pu
Description: When you are measuring the Electromagnetic torque Te of a Synchronous Machine SI Fundamental block through the measurement output m, the Te signal must be in N*m (since the machine units are SI). However, the value is returned in per unit (pu).
Workaround: You need to convert the measured elecrtomagnetic torque Te signal from pu to N*m units by multiplying the signal by a gain K, defined as: K = Pn / (2*pi*f / p) where Pn is the nominal power of the machine f is the nominal frequencyp is the number of pole paris of the machine

Summary: Generated code incorrectly includes header file for Lookup block replacement function in model.h instead of rtw_shared_utils.h
Description: Code generation for a model might place the #include  statement for a replacement function header file in the wrong generated header file if all of the following conditions exist: The model contains a Lookup block, or a variant such as a Prelookup block, that generates a utility function. The model parameter Shared code placement  is set to Shared location . Based on the target function library (TFL)/code replacement library (CRL) selected for the model, code generation replaces the Lookup block utility function with a TFL/CRL replacement function in the generated code for the model.  Under these conditions, the #include  statement for the header file associated with the TFL/CRL replacement function might be generated into model.h  rather than rtw_shared_utils.h .

Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting Run as administrator.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Change log:January 28, 2013: attachment_903907_11b_2013-01-28.zip, attachment_903907_12a_2013-01-28.zip: initial version, works on R2011b, R2012a onlyJanuary 29, 2012: Added attachment_903907_12b_2013-01-29.zip, works on R2012b only

Summary: MATLAB crashes during simulation while hovering in a Stateflow chart with debugging disabled
Description: MATLAB will crash while simulating a model with Stateflow charts if the following is true: global animation/debugging is enabled for the model, all the Stateflow charts in the model have animation/debugging disabled, and you hover over a Stateflow object inside the chart. If one or more of the charts in the model have animation/debugging enabled the issue will not occur and instead you will see "Data: unrecognized_symbol" in any chart that has debugging/animation disabled.
Workaround: You can use one of these three workarounds to avoid this issue: Disable debugging/animation for the entire model.     Click on Simulation in Menu Bar and then click on Model Configuration Parameters to open Configuration Parameters.    Go to Simulation Target and uncheck Enable debugging/animation to turn off the debugging for the entire model. Enable debugging for a Stateflow chart.    Open a chart for which debugging is disabled.   Click on Simulation in Menu Bar and then click on Debug and then check the Allow Breakpoints in this Chart option.  Do not hover over a Stateflow object while simulating the model.

Summary: Inconsistent conversion of out-of-range numbers passed to Java methods that take integers
Description: When calling Java methods, MATLAB conversion of some out-of-range integers and doubles passed as arguments to Java parameters declared as integers (long, int, short and byte) is not the same as similar conversions that would occur in a Java program.  An out-of-range number is one that cannot be represented within the range of values allowed by the type of the parameter, keeping in mind that Java does not have unsigned types. Specifically, for parameters declared as int, byte or short, MATLAB first converts the argument to a signed 2's complement 32-bit integer and then truncates the result to the appropriate number of bits.  For numbers greater than 231 -1 or less than -231 , which cannot be represented in a signed 32-bit integer, this first conversion will always yield the Java constant 0x80000000, which, when trimmed to the correct length, yields 0 for byte and short, and -231  (or -2,147,483,648) for int.  Similarly if the parameter is long, all values greater than 263 -1 or less than -263 , which cannot be implemented in a signed 64-bit integer, will be converted to -263  (or -9,223,372,036,854,775,808) which is equivalent to Java 0x80000000_00000000.   Java, on the other hand, converts out-of-range integers by using the appropriate number of low order bits of the integer's actual value, and it converts out-of-range doubles to the maximum or minimum value that can be represented in an int (for int, short or byte parameters) or long (for long parameters).
Workaround: If you need Java-style conversion for an argument whose value cannot be represented as a signed 32-bit integer (for int, short or byte parameters) or 64-bit integer (for long parameters), manipulate the bits using built-ins such as bitand and bitshift to adjust the value so that it is within range of an int or long, as appropriate.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier fails to return derived ranges in design error detection mode on certain Windows machines
Description: On certain Windows machines, Simulink Design Verifier fails to return derived ranges in design error detection mode. Deriving ranges in Simulink Design Verifier relies on Microsoft Visual C++ redistributable binaries. Installing these binaries will resolve the issue.
Workaround: Install both the Microsoft Visual C++ 2005 and 2008 redistributable packages following Microsoft's instructions at the links referenced below.Visual C++ 2005 SP1 redistributable package: http://www.microsoft.com/en-us/download/details.aspx?id=5638Visual C++ 2008 redistributable package:http://www.microsoft.com/en-us/download/details.aspx?id=29

Summary: MATLAB? scripts generated from discontiguous selections on systems without Excel? software import only the first selected block
Description: When importing spreadsheet data on systems without Microsoft? Excel? software, clicking Import Selection > Generate Script  in the Import Tool generates a script that imports only the data from the first selected block of a discontiguous selection. This does not match the behavior of the Import Data  option.
Workaround: If there are only a few discontiguous selected blocks, generate a separate script for each contiguous block of data and concatenate the scripts. Alternatively, edit the sub-reference expression for the raw variable on line 13 of the generated script to reflect the discontiguous selection. For example, the following expression imports data from rows 1 to 100 and 200 to 300 and columns 1 and 10: raw = raw([1:100,200:300],[1,10]);

Summary: Using bitwise operators may lead to compiler warning or assertion
Description: When the chart property "Enable C-bit operations" is selected, using the bitwise AND (& ) and bitwise OR (| ) in Stateflow C charts may cause warnings or assertions: Generated code may have compiler warnings like "possible loss of data"; Code generation process may have an assertion like "Types not equal: MatrixType::cast(aExpr->type())->base(), MatrixType::cast(aExpr->lhs()->type())->base()".  These situations happen when a binary bitwise operator (&  or | ) is used with operands of different types. For example, this expression:  y = y & u;   where y   is of type uint16  and u   is of type uint8 , will cause a problem.  Note that this situation can occur while using a bitwise NOT (~ ) operator. The NOT operator returns a type of the target integer, not the type of the operand. For example, if z   and w   are both uint8 , the following might cause a warning or assertion: z = z & ~w;   The first operand of & , the variable z , is of type uint8 . The second operand of & , the expression ~w , is of the type of the target integer (for example, int32 ). The code generation process will give an assertion only if the operands of the bitwise operation involve matrices of different base type. Otherwise, if the types of the operands are different, the generated code may have compiler warnings.
Workaround: Force the type of the operands of the bitwise operator to be identical by casting the operands. For example, z = int32(z) & ~w; instead of: z = z & ~w; or z &= ~w;.Note: The cast to int32 of z  is to cast it to the target integer type used. If you have a different target integer type,  for example int16, the cast should be adapted to that type.

Summary: The 1553 RT Send and RT Receive blocks do not work correctly if the message length mask contains hex2dec('ffffffff').
Description: In the example model, the message length masks inserted in the 1553 RT Initialize block are initialized to 0xffffffff. The RT Send and Receive blocks do not work correctly. The reason is an incorrect data type conversion that changes the message length mask value to 0x80000000, restricting the legal message length to 32 words.
Workaround: Unless a 32 word message length is required, leave the top bit set to 0 in the message length masks. To allow all message lengths except 32 words, set the mask to hex2dec('7fffffff').

Summary: Create code generation report option might cause code generation error
Description: On the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Code Generation > Report  pane, if you select the Create code generation report  check box, an error might occur during code generation: Error using str2StrVar (line 11) Assertion failed.
Workaround: Do one of the following:Locate files named tmp.m in your MATLAB search path by running which tmp.m at the command line. Rename any files found.On the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Code Generation > Debug pane, clear the Verbose build check box.

Summary: Reports, including the System Design Description (SDD) report, fail for some models with bus outputs
Description: Reports, including the System Design Description (SDD) report, can fail for some models that have a mix of bus and non-bus outputs if both  of these conditions exist: The model has at least two non-bus outputs whose execution order precedes that of a bus output.  The report include a Signal Loop component.  Generating a report from such a model causes the Report Generator to display an error dialog with the message "Cell elements must be character arrays."  ?

Summary: The "Processor-in-the-Loop (PIL) Simulation for Embedded Targets" section in documentation has missing steps for PIL simulation using xmakefilesetup.
Description: While executing PIL simulation steps described in the help section: "Processor-in-the-Loop (PIL) Simulation for Embedded Targets" for xmakefile setup configurations (ticcs_c2000_ccsv4 and ticcs_c2000_ccsv5), PIL simulation fails for the following reasons: A pause is required to give sufficient time to download the PIL project on the target before running the PIL simulation. The documentation does not mention that this pause is required.
Workaround: The text provided in the documentation section "Processor-in-the-Loop (PIL) Simulation for Embedded Targets > Running Your PIL Application to Perform Simulation and Verification" should be replaced with the following update:After you add your PIL block to your model, using the following command on the MATLAB command prompt, add the required pause needed for download in seconds:rtw.connectivity.Launcher.setStartApplicationPause(30)Click Simulation > Run or press Ctrl+T to run the PIL simulation and view the results.

Summary: For a model with simultaneous events, consecutive simulation runs might produce different results
Description: In SimEvents models, simultaneous events with the same event priorities might execute in a different order in consecutive simulation runs. This might cause each simulation run to produce different results. This issue might occur when ALL of the following conditions are true: In the SimEvents pane of the Model Configuration Parameters dialog box, Prevent duplicate events on multiport blocks and branched signals  is selected. Some blocks with reactive signal input ports are configured to schedule events with the same event priority. These reactive ports are connected to a common signal input. The update of the common signal input to which each reactive port connects is influenced by entity departures at an Entity Combiner block, an Entity Splitter block, or a Replicate block. ?
Workaround: For blocks that are configured with the same event priority value, change these parameter values so that they differ from each other.

Summary: Hyperlink to Rte_Type.h  does not work in code generation report
Description: On the code generation report for an AUTOSAR target, the hyperlink to file Rte_Type.h  does not work. The following error is generated: rtwtags: error opening '$Code Generation Directory$\Rte_Type.h'

Summary: Three examples are missing the Open This Example link in the documentation pages
Description: The following three examples are missing the Open This Example  link in their documentation pages. To open the example model, type the respective model name at the command prompt: Solid-Oxide Fuel Cell Connected to Three-Phase Electrical Power System - power_SOFC  Thyristor-Based HVDC Transmission System (Detailed Model) - power_hvdc12pulse  Thyristor-Based HVDC Transmission System (Average Model) - power_hvdc12pulse_average   ?
Workaround: To open the example model, manually type in the name of the  model at the command prompt.

Summary: Functions that launch file dialog boxes might return immediately without displaying the dialog box
Description: Deleting a figure and then calling a function that opens a file dialog box, such as uigetfile , uiputfile , or uigetdir , might fail to launch the dialog box on Windows? platforms. For example, the following code launches a MATLAB waitbar, deletes the waitbar dialog, and immediately launches a file dialog box. The uigetfile  function might return immediately without displaying the dialog box on the screen. f = waitbar(0,'foo'); delete(f); uigetfile;
Workaround: Flush the event queue after deleting a figure and before calling a function that opens a file dialog box. To flush the event queue, use drawnow or see the drawnow reference page for a list of other options.f = waitbar(0,'foo');delete(f);drawnow;uigetfile;

Summary: Quadruple click on Scope causes errors
Description: If you quadruple click on a Simulink Scope in a model, two scopes are launched. They will not be connected to Simulink properly and will cause errors.
Workaround: Double click on a Scope block to open it or use the attached patch.

Description: On some machines, when you try to open a file using the file open dialog, the dialog appears after a delay (~120 secs). Summary: Printing a multi-page document in Simulink to PDF results in a multiple requests to save a file
Description: Printing a Simulink model to PDF that requires multiple pages results in multiple print requests (one request for each page). To print the whole model to PDF, you must respond to each request, specifying a different file name.

Summary: Model checksums generated with the SDD report capability of the Simulink Report Generator?can differ from the checksums provided in the reference reports
Description: The model checksums generated with the System Design Description (SDD) report capability of the Simulink Report Generator??can differ from the checksums provided in the reference reports. The locally generated version of the model checksums depend on the execution environment and can be platform-specific. If the execution platform of the locally generated version is not the same as the execution platform used to generate the reference reports, some model checksums?can differ.
Workaround: In R2013a, for each platform, the DO Qualification Kit provides an automatically generated qualkitdo_rptgenext_checksums_ARCH.pdf file (ARCH is the execution platform 'glnxa64', 'maci64', 'win32', or 'win64'). For each execution platform, the file lists the checksums for all test models.Please refer the qualkitdo_rptgenext_checksums_ARCH.pdf file to get the correct checksums.When you regenerate the qualification reports for your platform, the checksums should match those provided in qualkitdo_rptgenext_checksums_ARCH.pdf.If you are a licensed DO Qualification Kit customer and would like a generated qualkitdo_rptgenext_checksums_ARCH.pdf file for an earlier release, please contact Mathworks support.

Summary: TeX fonts display smaller than expected in Simulink Editor
Description: The \fontsize  TeX command displays text smaller than the font size that you specify. ?
Workaround: Use \fontsize to increase the font size until the font displays at the size that you expected. Increasing the font size by a multiple of 4 usually produces the size that you want.

Summary: Simulation results might be incorrect for models that use struct arrays in Stateflow or MATLAB Function blocks
Description: If a model contains Stateflow or MATLAB Function blocks that read or output arrays of struct data, simulation might generate incorrect results.

Description: If you try to save a Simulink model or library in an SLX file in a ClearCase "dynamic view" (using the IBM Rational MultiVersion File System (MVFS)), then Simulink reports the error: boost::filesystem::last_write_time: Access is denied   Simulink does not save the model.After this error you cannot close the model, or save it elsewhere, and you must end the MATLAB process.

Summary: Code generation might fail for subsystem specified as Function with separate data
Description: If you select the Function with separate data  checkbox for a subsystem, a TLC error might occur during code generation of the model. The error message is similar to the following: Error: File: matlab\rtw\c\tlc\mw\opaquelib.tlc Line: 1509 Column: 51 Unable to find ContStatesVarGroupIndex within the System scope

Summary: Compiled standalones or libraries are slow to start up due to one byte read operations
Description: Deployed components generated from MATLAB Compiler or Builder products in releases R2012a to R2013a may have a slow start up time.  This can occur even after the initial run, when the Component Technology Archive (CTF) is already extracted, and the startup delay could be 5-6 times slower than the same code deployed from R2011b. If the components are on a network drive, further slowness and heavy use of network bandwidth may be an additional symptom.

Description: If you begin editing the name of a subchart at the chart level (from outside the subchart), the state actions inside the subchart might disappear. You do not need to actually change the name of the subchart for this issue to occur. Entering and exiting the name editing mode is all that's required.
