Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Inconsistent out-of-range handling for input signal of For Iterator block in simulation and code generation
Description: The For Iterator block converts its input signal value to an integer data type (either a type specified by parameter Iteration variable data type  or to a 32-bit signed integer by default). As a result, this conversion requires out-of-range handling for input signals that differ from the integer type. The handler mechanism used in simulation and generated code are inconsistent in the following ways causing a difference in simulation and code generation results: Case 1:  The input signal has an integer data type that causes an overflow (e.g. uint32 )In simulation, the conversion saturates on overflow, while in the generated code, the conversion wraps on overflow. Case 2:  The input signal is an floating point data type (e.g. double  or single )In simulation, the conversion saturates on integer overflow and uses Zero  integer rounding mode. In the generated code, the conversion wraps on integer overflow and uses Simplest  integer rounding mode. In addition, the generated code does not honor the Configuration Parameters  > Optimization > Remove code from floating-point to integer conversions that wraps out-of-range values  parameter. Specifically, the code that wraps out-of-range values for floating-point to integer conversion is always  removed. This eliminates the possibility of ensuring that the simulation and code generation results match.
Workaround: To work around this issue, design your system to ensure that for block inputs of integer data type, the input signal remains within the range of 32-bit signed integer (default conversion) or the user-specified integer output data type. It is preferable to avoid double or single precision input signal values to these two blocks and use integer types instead, to achieve clearer modeling semantics and simple generated code.

Summary: When marking a conflicted file resolved, using the Simulink Project's SVN integration, the operation can fail with the error message: "Could not resolved conflict for file [filename]".
Description: When you have a conflicted file under SVN it is not always possible to mark the conflict as resolved. When this failure happens the following error message is shown. "Could not resolved conflict for file [filename]" This is due to a bug in the SVN libraray which has now been fixed. Applying the patch will update your SVN library.
Workaround: To avoid this issue for new conflicts, install the patch below.The only way to resolve existing conflicts is to delete the conflicted file and update your sandbox.Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting Run as administrator.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Vector permutation of Selector block input signal
Description: A Selector block that specifies a permutation of its input signal might generate incorrect code.  For example, if an input signal A  is an 8-element vector and the Selector block is configured to produce A([5:8 1:4]) , the generated code might omit the permutation. For the error to occur, the total number of elements selected by the Selector block must equal the number of elements in the original signal.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block after the Selector block. Set the Signal Coversion block parameter Output to Signal copy.

Summary: Cannot generate "Testing a Wing Flutter Suppression System Design" example report
Description: On the Simulink Report Generator Examples page in the Help browser, for the "Testing a Wing Flutter Suppression System Design" example, clicking the Run Test  link in produces errors at the MATLAB command line. The first error is: No model found for simulation.
Workaround: You can view the report by clicking the "Open pregenerated report" link on the "Testing a Wing Flutter Suppression System Design" example page.

Summary: MATLAB can hang after update diagram for model including Stateflow chart and configured with unavailable code replacement library
Description: MATLAB can hang if all of the following conditions exist: Model contains a Stateflow chart. Model is configured for the code generator to use a code replacement library that is not registered or available on the MATLAB path. You  update the model diagram by selecting Simulation > Update Diagram  or pressing Ctlr+D .  If the condition occurs, Simulink reports an error, indicating that the code replacement library was not found, and then MATLAB hangs.
Workaround: Configure the model for the code generator to use a registered  code replacement library that is on the MATLAB path before updating the model diagram.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated AUTOSAR Version 4.0-compliant C code passes array arguments to RTE functions using incorrect level of indirection
Description: In AUTOSAR Version 4.0-compliant C code, an array argument should be passed to Run-Time Environment (RTE) functions as a pointer to the array base type. However, Embedded Coder incorrectly generates RTE function calls in which an array argument is passed as a pointer to the array type. For example, in R2012a, if you open the example model rtwdemo_autosar_multirunnables , set the AUTOSAR schema version to 4.0, export functions from subsystem ASWC, and inspect the generated file ASWC.c, the generated code for Runnable2  passes array argument rtb_Delay_a  using an incorrect level of indirection, as follows:   int8_T rtb_Delay_a[2];...  Rte_IWrite_Runnable2_Step_PPort_DE3(&rtb_Delay_a);

Summary: Infinite sorting of columns in the Fixed-Point Tool
Description: Certain actions in the Fixed-Point Tool can cause the tool to go into an infinite sorting loop. The tool will continuously cycle through sorting one column of data and then another. Most actions, such as saving your Simulink models, will be inaccessible until the loop is broken. The loop can be broken only by using the Fixed-Point Tool's View Manager. Infinite sorting of columns in the Fixed-Point Tool contents pane might occur after: Manually changing views in the Fixed-Point Tool Performing an action that causes a change in the view  For example, the following scenarios might cause this infinite sorting: Scenario 1:   In the Fixed-Point Tool: Simulate the model with Fixed-Point Instrumentation set to "MinMaxOverflow". By default, the Fixed-Point Tool displays the results sorted by the "Name" column. Propose data types and then sort the results by any column other than "Name". For example, the "Run" column. Simulate the model again.  Scenario 2:   In the Fixed-Point Tool: Simulate the model with Fixed-Point Instrumentation set to MinMaxAndOverflow. Sort the results in the current view by any column other than "Name". For example, sort by the "Run" column. Select a different view from the "Column view" menu. For example, if your selected "Column View" was "Simulation View", select "Automatic Data Typing View". Select the previous "Column View" In the previous example, select "Simulation View" again.  For both these scenarios,the Fixed-Point Tool goes into an infinite loop of sorting columns.
Workaround: There are two workarounds. The immediate workaround allows the current infinite loop to be broken, but does not prevent future infinite loops. The immediate workaround provides an opportunity to save all your work before exiting MATLAB. The second workaround permanently fixes the problem by overwriting files in your MATLAB installation. Immediate WorkaroundOpen the View Manager by clicking on the "Show Details" link next to the "Column View" drop down.Select all the column names in the right hand table and click on the 'X' button to delete all of the columns and stop the infinite sorting.Select "Options -> Manage Views... -> Options -> Reset All Views to Factory Settings..."Click "Yes" on the warning dialogClick OkInstallation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting Run as administrator.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Incorrect red COR check for dereference operation on pointer to void where pointer is a field in structure returned by stubbed function
Description: Consider the following code where a stubbed function returns a structure with a field that is a pointer to void . void foo(int x) {};  typedef struct {  int a;  int b;  int c;  void (*fptr)(int); } S;  extern void *fstub(int x);  void f(void) {  S *s = (S *)fstub(sizeof(S));  int x = s->a; /* orange which is OK */  s->fptr = foo; /* assigned a function to function pointer */  (s->fptr)(x); // red COR } Dereferencing this field leads to a red COR check, even though the field is correctly assigned to a function that exists. The check should at least be orange.
Workaround: Add a manual stub for the function fstub(), which calls malloc(): void *fstub(unsigned int x) { void *s = malloc(x);  return (void *)s;}Then rerun the verification with this definition in an external file. Instead of a red COR check, the verification generates a green COR check.

Summary: Nest Setup File component can cause a spurious context-checking error
Description: Moving a Nest Setup File component to a different context in a report outline can cause a spurious context-checking error, similar to the following: Component "Chapter" (section) may not contain "Nest" (text).Move "Nest" inside components of type paragaraph, table, or text. This can occur if you perform the following operations after inserting a Nest Setup File component in a report outline: Unselect the Nest Setup File component's Inline nested report in this report  option. Select the Insert link to external report  option. Select the Inline nested report in this report  option. Move the Nest Setup File component to a different context (for example, a child of a Chapter or Section component), if that new context is invalid for a text object.
Workaround: Perform the following steps:Unselect the Nest Setup File component's Inline nested report in this report option.Unselect the Insert link to external report option.Select the Inline nested report in this report option.

Summary: Message error: the size of an array must be greater than zero during C++ linking phase
Description: With certain code, if you specify the option -enum-type-definition auto-signed-first  or -enum-type-definition auto-unsigned-first , the Polyspace verification of C++ code may generate the following error during the linking phase: *** Beginning GNU C++ Linking Phase *** ********************************************************** Linking pst_file_internal (1/27) Linking pst_file_stub (2/27) pst_file_stub.c, line 447: error: the size of an array must be greater than zero | char chk___pst_t_13373[(sizeof(struct __pst_t_13373)==6) ? 1 : -1] ;  ?
Workaround: Specify the option -enum-type-definition defined-by-standard, and rerun the Polyspace verification.

Summary: Syntax highlighting incorrect for some strings with multibyte characters
Description: Stateflow labels that use certain types of character encoding might appear with incorrect syntax highlighting.
Workaround: Turn off syntax highlighting.

Summary: Simulink Preferences dialog does not keep the changes when you navigate the tree
Description: When you click a different tree item in the Simulink Preferences dialog box, any previous preference changes are lost.
Workaround: To avoid losing previous changes,  before moving to a different tree item, press Apply.

Summary: Possible assertion failure during MEX function generation due to memory integrity checks?
Description: During MEX function generation, a crash can result from a bug in the transformation that inserts run-time memory integrity checks around matrix expressions. In R2012b, on a Windows?  platform, this crash results in a stack trace output that includes the following line. In other releases or on other platforms, you might see a different path or line number. Assertion at .\dom_c\LowerMatrixTypesForBackend.cpp line 447: !MatrixType::isa(exprType)  ?
Workaround: If possible, upgrade to a later release. Otherwise, try to generate the MEX function with memory integrity checks disabled.To disable memory integrity checks:At the command line, in the code configuration object:    cfg = coder.config('mex');    cfg.IntegrityChecks = false;Using the MATLAB? Coder? app:On the Build tab, set Output type to MEX Function.Click More settings.On the Speed tab, clear the Ensure memory integrity check box.If you disable memory integrity checks, errors in the generated MEX function can cause memory corruptions.  Memory corruptions can result in unpredictable behavior, including crashing the MATLAB software.

Summary: Structural checksum for SimPowerSystems models is different for every model load
Description: When you open a model, that contains SimPowerSystems blocks, the checksum changes even though youmade no changes to your model before you closed it. The checksum change affects these workflows: SimState save/restore - You cannot use the SimState object to restore the model after the model has been closed and reopened. Accelerator rebuild - The accelerator code gen is triggered every time the model is reopened and run.

Summary: Reset input port of Gold Sequence Generator disappears after you run a simulation
Description: The Gold Sequence Generator block has a "Reset on nonzero input" option. If you enable this input option, a reset input port becomes available. After running a simulation, this reset input port may disappear. Disabling and enabling the "Reset on nonzero input" option does not correct the situation.

Summary: Parallel code generation of a model containing Model blocks in Normal mode may incorrectly cause an error
Description: Generating code for a model that meets all  of the following conditions may incorrectly result in an error message: The model contains Model blocks. You are building the code in parallel using the Parallel Computing Toolbox or MATLAB Distributed Computing Server. There is at least one Model block in Normal mode that references a model that use libraries.  The error is similar to this: {_Error using locUpdateMdlRefTarget (line 1041) Assertion failed. Error in toolbox\simulink\simulink\private\update_model_reference_targets.p>locUpdateMdlRefTargetWithCatch (line 1608)  [oStatus, oReason]= locUpdateMdlRefTarget(varargin{:});
Workaround: Set the Simulation Mode of all of the Model blocks in your model hierarchy to Accelerator.

Summary: PID Tuner graphical components missing
Description: When you open the PID Tuner from the MATLAB command window by entering the pidtool  command, occasionally some graphical components of the PID Tuner are missing. For example, the PID Tuner can be missing the tuning sliders or plot setting combo boxes. The same problem can occur when you open the PID Tuner from PID blocks by clicking the Tune  button in the block dialog box.
Workaround: To avoid this problem, enter the following command at MATLAB command prompt before running the PID Tuner:set(0,'visible','on');Then, open the PID Tuner.  ?

Summary: PID Tuner graphical components missing
Description: When you open the PID Tuner from PID blocks by clicking the Tune  button in the block dialog box, occasionally some graphical components of the PID Tuner are missing. For example, the PID Tuner can be missing the tuning sliders or plot setting combo boxes. The same problem can occur when you open the PID Tuner from the MATLAB command window by running the pidtool  command.
Workaround: To avoid this problem, enter the following command at MATLAB command prompt before running the PID Tuner:set(0,'visible','on');Then, open the PID Tuner.?

Summary: GUIDE/UICONTROL buttons don't display when deployed with MATLAB Builder JA.
Description: For a MATLAB Builder JA-generated Matlab GUI application. running the application on the deployment machines using Java and the MCR results in the GUIDE/UICONTROL buttons not displayed. In addition, any callback functions associated with pressing the buttons are not triggered. ?
Workaround: Add a command line argument -Dsun.awt.disableMixing=true to the javac command if using command line to run the Java driver class.If using an IDE, such as Eclipse, to build the Java driver class and run it, add: -Dsun.awt.disableMixing=true to the VM arguments list.This bug is caused by mixing heavyweight and lightweight components change which was originated in JRE 1.6.0, update 14. Adding system property -Dsun.awt.disableMixing=true to disable the heavyweight and lightweight mixing code can temporarily fix the problem. This workaround works for JRE 1.6, but not for JRE 1.7. Currently there is no workaround or fix for JRE1.7.

Summary: It is not possible to use both 1553 and ARINC CEI-520 blocks in the same model.
Description: The 1553 and ARINC CEI-520 libraries include entry points with the same names that perform slightly different functions. It is not possible to link a model that contains both sets of blocks.

Summary: Constant value block input sources can result in incorrect code.
Description: When the code generator reads constant data, the generator can access memory outside of the constant data storage. As a result, the generator may define constant values incorrectly in the generated code. This error is more likely to occur if the embedded target has no built-in 8 or 16-bit integer data types. The integer types are shown in the Configuration Parameters dialog box on the Hardware Implementation  pane. The problem has been observed on a 64-bit Windows host. ?
Workaround: Use 32-bit data types or a data type that is built-in to the hardware for constants or ground values.

Summary: uicontrols fail to respond to clicks when added to a figure while a separate, modal figure is open
Description: If a uicontrol is created in a figure while a modal figure is displaying, then that uicontrol fails to respond to user actions after the modal figure is dismissed.

Summary: Text fields and certain other components in the Signal Builder block are not readable on MAC OS 10.5
Description: Text fields and some other components in the Signal Builder block are not readable on MAC OS 10.5. The border seems to obscure some of the text in the widgets and make it unreadable. To reproduce this issue, open Simulink on a Macintosh running OS 10.5, and then drag and drop a Signal Builder block into a model. Double-click the block. Notice that the text fields are not readable.

Summary: The list of avilable fonts that the font chooser dialog displays when you right click on a graphics/ui object can be different from the list of available fonts that the properties pane displays.
Description: The list of avilable fonts that the font chooser dialog displays when you right click on a graphics/ui object can be different from the list of available fonts that the properties pane displays.

Summary: The MATLAB depfun function cannot find dependencies for parfor or spmd when called from a script file.
Description: depfun fails to find dependencies when invoked on a script file that calls spmd or parfor. For example, given the script file my_spmd.m:     spmd    labindex The command     depfun -toponly my_spmd fails to list spmd_feval.m as a dependency. Because of this failure, the MATLAB Compiler and Builder products cannot correctly compile script files that call spmd or parfor. Although the compilation appears to succeed and an application is successfully created, when run, the application generates an error message: Undefined variable "spmdlang" or class "spmdlang.spmd_feval_impl".
Workaround: To work around this error, convert the script MATLAB file (my_spmd.m, in this case) into a function MATLAB file by adding a function line at the top of the file;   function my_spmd     spmd     labindex

Summary: Properties added dynamically to an object do not appear in the Variable Editor
Description: If a property is added dynamically to an object that subclasses the dynamicprops  class, the new property does not appear in the Variable Editor.
Workaround: Access the list of properties in the object from the MATLAB? Command Window.

Summary: SISOTOOL Bode Editor text displays incorrectly while dragging curves on MAC platform
Description: When you click and drag a curve in the SISOTOOL Bode Editor, the text for G.M., Freq, and P.M. does not display correctly. While you are dragging the curve, the updated text overlaps with the older text and the display becomes a dark patch. After you finish interacting with the curve, the text is properly redrawn with the updated values.

Summary: Error when docking and undocking figures in a multiple monitor setup
Description: With a multiple monitor setup, if a figure window is created and placed in one monitor and then docked or undocked into a different monitor, MATLAB can throw the following exception: java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: adding a container to a container on a different GraphicsDevice
